supporting_evidence: The iconic silhouette of the Bates house against the dark sky, emphasizing its menacing presence through light and shadow.
The use of harsh lighting and deep shadows within the Bates Motel parlor, creating a claustrophobic and unsettling atmosphere around Norman Bates and his taxidermy.
The rapid-fire editing and stark visual contrasts during the infamous shower scene, which uses the absence of color to heighten the terror and ambiguity.
The overall cinematography consistently employs high contrast and dramatic lighting for suspense and psychological effect throughout the film.
